Activate the dormant S3 baseline plumbing in app/drain_analyzer.py. Every needed function already exists -- they just need a scheduler, a boot-time restore call, and three operator-facing endpoints.

Why: a fresh pod has no way to catch up to the last known-good baseline. An on-call engineer who knows the system is healthy can't tell Drain3 "learn from this moment."

Goals:
- Boot-time download_baseline_from_s3() if local persistence empty AND bucket configured
- APScheduler in-process upload_snapshot_to_s3() at configurable interval
- Three operator endpoints: POST /drain3/snapshot, POST /drain3/baseline/tag, POST /drain3/baseline/restore

Closes the structural gap exposed by the 2026-04-29 HighKongP95Latency 0b215ef3 incident along two dimensions: MCP-only data access (every data source the LLM sees must come through an MCP bridge -- no direct DB / Prometheus / in-process reads) and trace depth (drill into per-span attributes via the existing get_trace MCP tool that was never wired up).

Why: the 0b215ef3 decision emailed kubectl set resources --limits=memory=2Gi + kubectl rollout restart deploy/spring-boot as remediation for a Kong p95 latency alert. Investigation found three structural causes: (a) the F-4 confidence clamp adjusted the trust signal but didn't strip templated actions; (b) the pipeline never called get_trace(trace_id) so the LLM saw only service-boundary trace summaries; (c) several code paths read data directly (entity_baselines -> Prometheus, bounded_agency -> SQLite, exemplars -> in-process import), bypassing the MCP hallucination firewall.

MCP-only invariant (durable architectural rule): every data source the LLM sees must come through an MCP bridge. Direct httpx/requests/DB calls are forbidden outside the MCP servers themselves and the sanctioned _mcp_call helper. Boot-time/migration code is exempt -- it never feeds the LLM.

Tier mapping:
- Tier 0 (S3-HF-01): F-4 clamp strips actions -- ships immediately, ~45 min
- Tier 1 (S3-HF-07): trace drill via get_trace MCP -- the structural fix
- Tier 2 (S3-HF-03): hypothesis-menu validator -- backstop
- Tier 4 (S3-HF-09): chaos scorer 5th axis + corpus seed + CI gate -- measurement scaffold for Sprint 4
- MCP integrity (S3-HF-04, 05, 06, 08): four code-path refactors + a CI lint
- Bug (S3-HF-02): defensive template-lookup tests; actual root cause was LLM hallucination neutralized by S3-HF-01

Out of scope (deferred to Sprint 4):
- Tier 3 -- Iterative agentic gather (hypothesis-tree state, 3-5 day rewrite of bounded_agency.py). Gates on Sprint 3's Tier 4 measurement scaffold being green first.

Summary: Extend feedback schema + endpoint + 4 MCP tools
Description:
As an operator, I need to thumbs-up/down individual decisions and tag what was wrong, so future RCAs can learn from my feedback.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Schema migration in app/rca_store.py:_init_schema (idempotent ALTER TABLE):
  - feedback ADD COLUMN rating TEXT (CHECK rating IN ('up','down') OR NULL)
  - feedback ADD COLUMN tags TEXT (JSON array, only when rating='down')
  - feedback ADD COLUMN actual_root_cause TEXT (only when rating='down', optional)
  - feedback ADD COLUMN comment TEXT
  - feedback ADD COLUMN rater TEXT
- New endpoint POST /decisions/{id}/feedback with RatingFeedbackRequest Pydantic schema
- Validation: tags + actual_root_cause rejected when rating=='up'; tags must be subset of 5 allowed enum values (wrong_severity, wrong_root_cause, wrong_evidence, wrong_action, missing_context)

- Existing US-5.3 endpoints unaffected; feedback_type stays for the override/confirm path

Files: app/rca_store.py, app/models.py, app/main.py, tests/test_feedback_rating.py, monitoring-mcp-servers/rca_history_mcp/main.py.

Summary: Tier 0 -- F-4 clamp strips actions + emits diagnostic_steps
Description:
As an SRE, I need the F-4 confidence clamp to strip templated/untrustworthy suggested_actions and replace them with read-only diagnostic verbs, so a clamped 0.40 decision can never email kubectl rollout/scale/set commands.

Trigger: 2026-04-29 HighKongP95Latency 0b215ef3 incident emailed kubectl set resources --limits=memory=2Gi + kubectl rollout restart deploy/spring-boot at 0.40 confidence. F-4 had clamped confidence but did NOT strip the bad actions.

Acceptance Criteria:
- LLMDecision gains diagnostic_steps: list[str] field with the existing list-coercion validator
- RCARecord gains diagnostic_steps: Optional[str] (JSON list)
- New app/clamp_actions.py module with diagnostic_steps_for_clamp(alert, rca, quality, actions_source) -> list[str]:
  - Alert-aware: HighKongP95Latency -> upstream-vs-proxy PromQL pivot; HighP95Latency on spring-boot -> hikari + JVM GC pivots; *MemoryUsage/PodHigh* -> topk container_memory pivot; TargetDown -> kube events pivot; Drain3AnomalyDetected -> read template lines (no metric invention)
  - Always ends with explicit "Do NOT run kubectl rollout/scale/set on `{service}` until a specific cause is named"
  - Read-only: no kubectl rollout/scale/set/patch/delete, no systemctl restart, no docker restart
- pipeline.py Step 6d: when clamp fires, also strip suggested_actions and populate diagnostic_steps. Evidence preserved verbatim so quality classifier doesn't drop to needs_review
- pipeline.py Step 7: persist diagnostic_steps in RCARecord
- rca_store.py: column migration + INSERT slot + JSON-decode at /decisions API boundary
- notifier.py: new "Diagnostic steps -- read-only; investigate before remediating" card; "Withheld" explanation in Suggested actions card when clamp stripped them
- Tests: 24 new tests across tests/test_clamp_actions.py (8), tests/test_pipeline_clamp.py (6), tests/test_action_templates.py (5 defensive), tests/test_confidence_clamp_f4.py (5 unchanged for legacy path)

Validation case: replay decision 0b215ef3-74fa-4e1f-88a4-057437f04d0e through patched pipeline. Assert: confidence=0.40, suggested_actions=[], diagnostic_steps[0].startswith("Open Jaeger"), no kubectl substring anywhere.

Shipped 2026-04-29 in commit b0f1d0c on monitoring-triage-service:main. Suite 178 -> 204, zero regressions.

Summary: Defensive tests for HighKongP95Latency template lookup
Description:
Originally framed as "bug: HighKongP95Latency wrong-archetype lookup" after the 0b215ef3 incident emailed kubectl set resources --limits=memory=2Gi + kubectl rollout restart deploy/spring-boot. Investigation 2026-04-29 confirmed the template lookup is FINE: HighKongP95Latency rule at suggested_actions.yaml line 133 returns Kong-targeting actions; the OOMKill template at line 64-77 only matches ^(High|Medium|Critical)MemoryUsage$. The bad 0b215ef3 actions came from the LLM directly (decision.suggested_actions was non-empty so fill_template never fired).

Reframed as defensive tests: pin the regex boundary so future YAML edits can't introduce wrong-archetype keying. Actual remediation for LLM-side bad actions lives in S3-HF-01 (Tier 0 strip).

Acceptance Criteria:
- New tests/test_action_templates.py with 5 defensive tests:
  - test_high_kong_p95_latency_returns_kong_targeted_actions: HighKongP95Latency MUST target kong-kong, NOT contain memory=2Gi --requests=memory=1Gi signature
  - test_high_memory_usage_returns_oomkill_template: confirm OOMKill template fires for HighMemoryUsage (its actual alertname)
  - test_high_p95_latency_first_action_is_not_unconditional_memory_bump: first action is rollout restart (connection pool play); memory bump must be conditional ('If ...')
  - test_oomkill_template_does_not_match_kong_or_latency_alertnames: regex boundary pin (HighKongP95Latency, HighP95Latency, HighMemoryUsageButNotReally, MyHighMemoryUsage all rejected)
  - test_high_kong_p95_does_not_target_spring_boot_in_template: defense in depth across service label variants

Shipped 2026-04-29 in commit b0f1d0c.

As an SRE, I need the validator to reject RCAs that list possibilities without committing ("possibly X or Y", "may be due to") and RCAs whose named cause shares no token with the evidence list, so the LLM is forced to commit to one diagnosis or admit data starvation.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Add _HYPOTHESIS_MENU_PATTERNS to app/response_validator.py:
  - "possibly X or Y", "either A or B", "may be due to (a slow query|pool saturation|GC)", "could be one of"
  - Avoid FP on legitimate "or"-prose by requiring 5+ words on either side
- Add cause-must-share-token-with-evidence rule:
  - Tokenize RCA's first sentence (4+ char words, lowercased)
  - Tokenize decision.evidence list
  - Fail if intersection minus stopwords (this/that/with/from/have/service/alert/metric) is empty
- Both rules ship aggressive behind feature flag triage_hypothesis_menu_strict (default True)
- New Prometheus metric triage_validator_retries_total{reason} so we can dial back if FP rate exceeds 5%
- Hits trigger the existing retry path (pipeline.py:444-566); Tier 0 clamp (S3-HF-01) is the safety net for retries that still fail
- 8+ new test cases in tests/test_response_validator.py covering FP/TP boundaries

Validation case: replay 0b215ef3 RCA -> should_retry=True due to "hypothesis-menu" hit on "possibly a regressed query or saturated connection pool".

Summary: MCP integrity -- Route bounded_agency rca_history through MCP
Description:
As an architect, I need bounded_agency.py to route ALL its data lookups through MCP servers -- no direct DB queries, no in-process module imports -- so the LLM never sees data that didn't pass through the hallucination firewall.

Trigger: app/bounded_agency.py:222-242 currently:
- rca_history.similar -> direct store.get_recent_decisions_for_alert() (DB)
- rca_history.list_exemplars -> direct "from app import exemplars" (in-process)
- rca_history.get_exemplar -> same direct in-process import

Acceptance Criteria:
- Refactor all three to HTTP calls against rca_history_mcp on port 8095
- For rca_history.similar: use the new get_similar_decisions() tool from S3-HF-06
- For list_exemplars/get_exemplar: add corresponding tools to rca_history_mcp (move app/exemplars/ data into a location the MCP server can load, OR expose a read-only API on the triage service that the MCP wraps -- pick whichever is cleaner during implementation)
- After this lands, the entire bounded_agency.py tool whitelist routes through MCP servers exclusively

Files: app/bounded_agency.py, monitoring-mcp-servers/rca_history_mcp/main.py (new exemplar tools), tests.

Done when: zero direct store.* or "from app import exemplars" calls remain in bounded_agency.py; existing bounded-agency tests pass against MCP-routed paths.

Summary: Tier 1 -- Deeper trace gather via get_trace MCP
Description:
As the LLM, I need to see span-level trace details (operation, db.statement, http.target, error tags) when a latency alert fires, not just service-boundary span summaries -- so I can name a specific cause inside the slow service instead of producing a hypothesis menu.

Trigger: 0b215ef3 RCA correctly identified "Kong span 3ms vs upstream span 8200ms" but couldn't name what was slow inside spring-boot. The pipeline never called the existing jaeger_mcp /tools/get_trace endpoint -- it stops at find_traces summary.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Extend app/context.py:_fetch_jaeger to call jaeger_mcp /tools/get_trace on top-K slowest + any error traces (default K=3, parallel via asyncio.gather)
- Add new models in app/models.py:
  - TraceSpanSummary: trace_id, span_id, operation, service, duration_ms, parent_operation, db_statement (truncated 200 chars), http_target, http_status, error
  - TraceDrillResult: trace_id, total_duration_ms, span_count, dominant_service, dominant_service_pct, slowest_span, db_call_count, db_total_ms, has_errors
  - GatheredContext gains trace_drills: Optional[list[TraceDrillResult]]
- New prompt section in app/llm_client.py rendering: dominant_service + percentage + db_call_count + db_total_ms, plus the slowest span line with operation + db.statement (if present, truncated) + http.target + status + error flag
- Pre-implementation step (per Q5=B): sample 10 spring-boot traces in production via curl http://jaeger-mcp:8094/tools/get_trace?trace_id=..., eyeball db.statement values to confirm JDBC parameterization. Default assumption: parameterized -- no PII redaction needed. If literal values appear: add a regex redactor stripping numeric literals (\\d{3,}), email-shaped tokens, UUIDs before db.statement field lands in evidence/email
- New settings: jaeger_drill_top_k, jaeger_drill_min_duration_ms

Files: app/context.py (_fetch_jaeger + new helpers _pick_drill_candidates, _summarize_trace), app/models.py (new dataclasses), app/llm_client.py (prompt section), app/config.py, tests/test_context_jaeger_drill.py.

Done when: replay 0b215ef3 window -- prompt sent to LLM includes a "Trace span breakdown" section with "dominant=spring-boot 99%, db_calls=N, db_total=Xms, slowest span: SELECT ... = Yms". A new chaos test high_p95_latency (Sprint 4) will be the end-to-end verifier.

Summary: MCP integrity -- CI lint forbidding direct data access
Description:
As an architect, I need a CI check that fails any PR adding direct httpx/requests/aiosqlite calls outside the MCP boundaries, so the MCP-only invariant survives team turnover.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Add CI check (ruff custom rule, semgrep pattern, or grep-based pre-commit) forbidding httpx/requests/aiosqlite/raw-SQL calls outside allowed boundaries:
  - Allowed: monitoring-mcp-servers/* (the MCP layer itself), app/context.py:_mcp_call (the only sanctioned bridge from triage-service to MCPs)
- Exempt boundary (per Q3=B): lifespan/startup/migration code -- app/main.py:lifespan, app/rca_store.py:_init_schema, drain3 boot restore in S3-DR-01 (download_baseline_from_s3). These are operator-tier and never feed the LLM
- Forbidden everywhere else: any module that runs during request handling and could reach the LLM prompt
- Document the rule in a new monitoring-docs/architectural-invariants.md page so it survives team turnover
- Add to GitHub Actions workflow for monitoring-triage-service + monitoring-mcp-servers

Files: .github/workflows/lint.yml (or equivalent), pyproject.toml (ruff config), monitoring-docs/architectural-invariants.md (new doc), pre-commit hook.

Done when: CI fails the build when a PR adds a forbidden direct call; a deliberate test PR with httpx call in app/pipeline.py is rejected.

Summary: Tier 4 -- Chaos scorer 5th axis + corpus seed + CI gate
Description:
As an SRE, I need a binary "did the RCA name the actual injected cause?" score on every chaos run, plus a CI regression gate against a seeded corpus of bad decisions, so Sprint 4's Tier 3 agentic loop can be measured rather than shipped blind.

Why this must land BEFORE Sprint 4's Tier 3: Tier 3 (iterative agentic loop with hypothesis-tree state) is a high-cost high-risk change. Without a binary metric and a corpus regression gate, Tier 3 ships unmeasured. Tier 4 is the scoreboard.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Extend monitoring-project/scripts/chaos/lib/rca_scorer.py with a 5th binary axis cited_injected_cause (0/1)
- Add expected_cause_tokens: list[str] to monitoring-project/scripts/chaos/tests/base.py (ChaosTest base class). Score 1.0 if any token appears in decision.rca_report or decision.evidence; 0.0 otherwise
- Seed monitoring-triage-service/tests/corpus/labeled/bad/hypothesis_only_clamp_leak.json with the failed 0b215ef3 decision JSON:
  - Full payload + expected_violations (hypothesis-menu, cause-evidence-mismatch from S3-HF-03)
  - expected_post_clamp_actions_must_not_contain (kubectl rollout, kubectl set resources)
  - expected_post_clamp_actions_must_contain_any_of (Open Jaeger, hikaricp_connections_active, jvm_gc_pause_seconds)
- Add monitoring-triage-service/tests/test_corpus_regression.py: every JSON in corpus/labeled/bad/* must trigger >=1 violation from validate()
- Wire into CI as a regression gate

Files: monitoring-project/scripts/chaos/lib/rca_scorer.py, monitoring-project/scripts/chaos/tests/base.py, monitoring-triage-service/tests/corpus/labeled/bad/hypothesis_only_clamp_leak.json (new seed), monitoring-triage-service/tests/test_corpus_regression.py, CI config.

Done when: chaos report shows new cited_injected_cause column with binary pass/fail per test row. A deliberate test PR weakening the validator (removing the hypothesis-menu pattern) fails the corpus regression gate.
